title: View Timeline, Liquid Glass, 3D Cylinder, Spotlight, 3D Carousel
description: Five live UI patterns: CSS scroll-driven entrance, liquid glass refraction, 3D cylinder typography, spotlight cursor, reactive 3D carousel.

drops.title: View Timeline Entrance
drops.description: CSS animation-timeline: view() fires a 3D perspective entrance as cards enter the viewport — zero JavaScript.
drops.concept: The CSS Scroll-Driven Animations spec lets elements animate relative to their position in the viewport via animation-timeline: view(). Combined with animation-range: entry, each card triggers a rotateX(30deg) + blur + scale entrance the moment it enters — no IntersectionObserver, no requestAnimationFrame, pure CSS.
drops.source: web.dev Scroll-driven Animations guide + MDN CSS scroll-driven animations

interaction.scroll: Scroll down to trigger card entrances
interaction.mouse: Hover cards to see border highlight

drops.title: Liquid Glass Refraction
drops.description: SVG feDisplacementMap via backdrop-filter physically bends background pixels — the optical effect that swept the web after WWDC 2025.
drops.concept: Apple debuted Liquid Glass at WWDC 2025, encoding real optical refraction (Snell's Law) into an SVG displacement map applied via backdrop-filter. Unlike flat glassmorphism blur, this distorts the pixels behind the panel by 8-14px per axis, creating a true convex-lens look. A CSS ::before specular gradient adds the rim highlight.
drops.source: kube.io liquid-glass-css-svg + Apple WWDC 2025 Liquid Glass

interaction.mouse: Hover glass cards for 3D tilt parallax

drops.title: 3D Cylinder Text
drops.description: Words placed around spinning 3D cylinders using trig math, CSS preserve-3d, and backface-visibility:hidden.
drops.concept: Each word is positioned with translate3d(x,y,z) + rotateX/Y computed from sin/cos of its angular index — placing it on a circle in 3D space. backface-visibility:hidden hides words facing away. Drag to spin; three modes: Cylinder (Y-axis ring), Double Ring (two counter-rotating rings), and Tunnel (Z-depth recession). All in pure CSS + vanilla trig math.
drops.source: David Faure / Codrops 3D Text Scroll Animation (Nov 2025)

drops.title: CSS Spotlight Hover
drops.description: A cursor-following radial gradient punches a spotlight hole through a dark overlay using only 2 lines of JavaScript.
drops.concept: A fixed overlay element holds a radial-gradient with a transparent center whose position is controlled by --cx and --cy CSS custom properties. The mousemove handler writes these two properties — that's the entire JavaScript. Reveal mode shows content, Torch mode darkens the cursor area. Radius and softness are tunable via sliders.
drops.source: Amit Sheen / Frontend Masters 'CSS Spotlight Effect' (May 2025)

interaction.click: Switch between Reveal / Torch / Off modes

drops.title: 3D Reactive Carousel
drops.description: 3D perspective carousel where the ambient background canvas interpolates toward the active card's dominant color palette.
drops.concept: Cards are positioned with translate3d + rotateY computed from distance to center — the active card faces forward at full scale while edges recede and tilt. A canvas behind the carousel renders multi-blob radial gradients that smoothly lerp toward the active card's RGB palette on each frame, creating an Apple TV-style ambient halo effect. Drag or keyboard to navigate.
